sourceMovie.title: Heart of Stone
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Heart of Stone works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Police Story 4: First Strike
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heart of Stone clicked for you, Police Story 4: First Strike (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Canary Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heart of Stone clicked for you, Canary Black (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heart of Stone clicked for you, The Terminator (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Heart of Stone?
faq.answer: Try Police Story 4: First Strike, Canary Black, The Terminator—each shares DNA with Heart of Stone while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Heart of Stone?
faq.answer: Heart of Stone (2023) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Heart of Stone worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
